styleGuide.approach: hand-inked illustrated icons in the style of a homesteader's field journal — ink line + soft watercolor wash, slight paper grain, transparent background. Each icon should read as a single tactile object you could pick up off a wooden game board.
paletteConstraints: anchor palette: walnut brown #4b2e1f, parchment cream #efe1c0, moss green #5b6b3a, ochre #c08a3e, bone #f4ead2, muted berry red #8a2f2a, charcoal #2a241f, dull metal #8a8275
no neon, no glossy highlights, no pure black or pure white
use the muted berry red sparingly, only for fruit and blood-warm accents
composition: centered subject with a small amount of breathing room
consistent light from top-left, soft warm shadow at lower-right
subtle off-axis tilt allowed, like a sketch placed on a card
optional faint paper-grain texture inside the silhouette, none outside it
transparent background — never render parchment or wood behind the subject (the UI panel supplies that)
consistency: uniform ink line weight across all icons (~3px at 256 res)
all icons share the same ink color (#2a241f) and the same wash saturation level
tools in the same tier ladder must share haft style, lashing style, and head silhouette — only the head material/color changes between tiers
all ingots share the same brick shape; only color changes
all ores share the same chunky stone silhouette; only the embedded mineral color changes
stations are drawn at the same three-quarter angle so they read as a set

phaseTinting.autumn: deeper ochre, dusk red, browned greens

icons.description: horizontal split log with two visible growth rings and rough bark on the ends
